LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA FISH AND GAME COMMISSION AND DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND GAME'S DEMURRER OF RESPONDENTS TO PETITIONER'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3, OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 BECAUSE PETITIONER HAS STATED A CLAIM THAT RESPONDENT HAS FAILED TO PROPERLY CARRY OUT ITS MANDATORY DUTY SEEKING PEER REVIEW. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER ON A REASONABLE SCHEDULE TO PROCEED WITH THE CASE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Petitioner'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs is off calendar per stipulation filed by parties June 13, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Clarification Of Timing And Scope Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ERIN TOBIN, ESQ., 415.217.2000, FOR PETITIONER THE CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY.
DANIEL HARRIS, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 415.703.5530, FOR RESPONDENTSCALIFORNIA FISH AND GAME COMMISSION AND CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND GAME.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its modified tentative ruling: The Commission acted in accordance with the writ and judgment by setting aside the Notice of Findings at its November 2012 meeting and that, as a result of doing so, the fisher is restored to the list of candidate species per the Commission's prior decision without need for any further action on the part of the Commission. cOUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. Judge: HAROLD KAHN, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
